fixed_mask_no_pad.interpretation: Highest naive-linkability — same plaintext maps to identical GPU tensor.

fresh_mask_no_pad.interpretation: Fresh mask alone removes the identity equivalence but preserves linear structure.

fixed_mask_fresh_pad.interpretation: Fresh pad alone scrambles values but reuses the same mask across requests.

fresh_mask_fresh_pad.interpretation: Fresh mask + fresh pad — lowest naive-linkability under this proxy.

pad_linkability_proxy.summary_note: fixed_mask_no_pad is the highest naive-linkability risk; fresh pad reduces the stability of the GPU-visible tensor across requests with identical plaintext input. This is a proxy under a naive observer model only, not a security proof.

per_mask.leakage_note: Only fingerprint counts are recorded; no mask tensor contents are emitted.
Only fingerprint counts are recorded; no mask tensor contents are emitted.
Only fingerprint counts are recorded; no mask tensor contents are emitted.
Only fingerprint counts are recorded; no mask tensor contents are emitted.
Only fingerprint counts are recorded; no mask tensor contents are emitted.

mask_freshness_audit.summary_note: Only sha256 fingerprints of mask tensors are kept. The condition number stats are aggregate-only and do not expose any single mask. Unexpected reuse counts duplicates outside the declared session-reuse policy.

gpu_visible.name: obfuscated_input
gpu_visible.leakage_note: X_tilde = (X - T) N_in or X N_in — never the plaintext input.

gpu_visible.name: transformed_linear_weight
gpu_visible.leakage_note: W_tilde = N_in_inv W N_out — depends on masks only.

gpu_visible.name: transformed_lora_adapter
gpu_visible.leakage_note: A_tilde, B_tilde — adapter content depends on rank mask.

gpu_visible.name: compensation_terms
gpu_visible.leakage_note: C = T W N_out is GPU-visible; depends on pad and masks, not on plaintext X.

gpu_visible.name: obfuscated_kv_cache
gpu_visible.leakage_note: Stage 4.8 ObfuscatedGPT2KVCache: GPU keeps only masked K/V.

gpu_visible.name: obfuscated_encoder_memory_cache
gpu_visible.leakage_note: Stage 6.2 EncoderMemoryCache (probe-only) — GPU never sees K_plain/V_plain.

gpu_visible.name: obfuscated_logits
gpu_visible.leakage_note: logits_tilde = logits N_vocab; vocab mask is diagonal in Stage 4.7+.

trusted_only.name: plaintext_hidden_state
trusted_only.leakage_note: Intermediate plaintext hidden states stay on trusted side.

trusted_only.name: plaintext_logits
trusted_only.leakage_note: Recovered logits Y = Y_tilde N_out_inv stay trusted-only.

trusted_only.name: sampling_result
trusted_only.leakage_note: Argmax / token id produced by greedy decode is trusted-only.

trusted_only.name: masks
trusted_only.leakage_note: N_in, N_out, N_Q, N_K, N_V, N_vocab never sent to GPU.

trusted_only.name: mask_inverses
trusted_only.leakage_note: N_*_inv held by SimulatedTEE for input transform and output recovery.

trusted_only.name: pads
trusted_only.leakage_note: T held trusted-only; only T W N_out is sent across as compensation.

trusted_only.name: plaintext_lora_adapter
trusted_only.leakage_note: Plain LoRA A, B held trusted-only; only A_tilde, B_tilde cross to GPU.

trusted_only.name: optimizer_state
trusted_only.leakage_note: No optimizer state crosses the boundary in this inference-only stage.

summary_notes: security proxy does not prove semantic security.
real TEE isolation is not implemented in this stage.

cache_leakage_proxy.interpretation_note: This is a direct nearest-neighbour matching proxy under cosine similarity. It does not implement adaptive or learned inversion attacks. A low top1_match_rate for obfuscated→plain only bounds the naive observer; the absence of a stronger attack here is not a guarantee.

global_limitations: These experiments are security proxies, not formal security proofs.
They do not implement adaptive attacks.
They do not implement learned inversion attacks.
They do not evaluate real TEE isolation.
They do not cover side channels.
They do not prove LoRA adapter extraction resistance.
